f1514fe1-d1e5-44e1-a046-8b3797f5c8a4.description: Integrated aromas of black fruits and a consistent palate, with slight herbal hints emerging on the finish. The subtle, well-executed perfume of the wood is present again, with grippy tannins and a touch of stem influence.
f1514fe1-d1e5-44e1-a046-8b3797f5c8a4.rationale:
Core Value Proposition

    Performance: Consistently the highest-rated Malbec in the current portfolio (96-99 points from major critics).

    Yield/Scarcity: Sourced from a specific, high-altitude 2.5-hectare plot in Agrelo; limited annual case allocation.

    Brand Authority: Winemaker Alejandro Vigil (Chief Winemaker at Catena Zapata) holds the first 100-point score for Argentine Cabernet Franc and Chardonnay.

Market Positioning for the Balkans

    Sommelier Entry: High-spec technical data and critical acclaim provide the necessary leverage for placement in "Grand Opening" or Michelin-standard wine lists.

    Style Profile: Unlike high-alcohol, oak-heavy Malbecs, "As Bravas" focuses on acidity, mineral tension, and herbal notes, aligning with the "Old World" palate preferences prevalent in Southeast Europe.

    Price-to-Prestige Ratio: Competes directly with Top-Growth Bordeaux or Super Tuscans at a lower acquisition cost, protecting margins for on-trade partners.

09e2e439-5c9d-4d52-888a-4f67e8b6a36d.description:
Compact aromas, predominantly black fruits. Juicy and broad mouthfeel with consistent textures.

A frank palate with everything in its place; freshness, fine tannins, and body. Conceptually, it is a wine that clearly understands its identity. Black fruit notes and delicate floral tones prevail on the finish, demonstrating how well it adapted to the vintage.
09e2e439-5c9d-4d52-888a-4f67e8b6a36d.rationale:
Core Value Proposition

    Critical Benchmark: Achieved a perfect 100-point score (Tim Atkin MW), establishing it as a definitive global reference for "mountain wine."

    Terroir Precision: Produced from a specific 0.8-hectare parcel in Paraje Altamira, characterized by "super-calcareous" soil (large stones covered in calcium carbonate).

    Technical Purity: Fermented and aged entirely in concrete eggs to eliminate oak influence, emphasizing raw soil expression and chalky texture.

Market Positioning for the Balkans

    Investment Grade: The 2020 vintage serves as a high-liquidity asset for private collectors and luxury cellars in coastal Croatia, Montenegro, and Belgrade.

    Palate Alignment: The high-altitude acidity and intense salinity mirror the structural profile of premium European whites and "Grand Cru" reds, appealing to the traditionalist Balkan palate.

    Exclusivity Factor: Extremely low production volume; its presence on a wine list functions as a "halo product," elevating the perceived authority of the venue's entire South American selection.

a2cb6cba-dd9f-49df-ace2-bd961c0a6a36.description: Aromas of combustion and a hint of phosphorus, beyond the white fruits. Showing certain breadth on the palate and complex nuances, with notes of dried honey, dried fruits, and slight hints of botrytis. No lees stirring (battonage), but two years on the lees in foudre. According to the winemaker, the first year is about fruit and the second is when gunpowder appears, adding complexity and roundness.
a2cb6cba-dd9f-49df-ace2-bd961c0a6a36.rationale:
Core Value Proposition

    The "Sake-Adjacent" White: A unique expression of Chenin Blanc from 50-year-old vines in Villa Seca. Features a dual-phase vinification: 10 days of skin contact followed by aging in large used French oak foudres, resulting in a wine of exceptional texture and "umami" depth.

    High Precision/Low Alcohol: Clocking in at a remarkably lean 11.1% ABV, it offers incredible energy and weight without the heavy alcohol footprint, aligning perfectly with the Japanese preference for elegance and balance.

    Critical Acclaim: Consistently rated 94–96 points (Descorchados, Tim Atkin), it is recognized as a pioneer of "New Wave" Argentine whites.

Market Positioning for Japan (Washoku Focus)

    Umami Integration: The skin-contact phase and extended lees aging provide a savory, lactic, and earthy character that mirrors the complexity of premium Junmai Ginjo sake. It complements the fermented elements of Japanese cuisine (miso, shoyu) rather than competing with them.

    Gastronomic Precision: Its "laser-sharp" natural acidity acts as a palate cleanser for fatty O-toro (tuna) or high-spec Tempura, cutting through richness while the saline/rock salt finish enhances the natural sweetness of raw shellfish.

    The "New World" Alternative to Koshu: For sommeliers looking to bridge the gap between local Japanese Koshu and international varieties, "Propósitos" offers a familiar mineral-driven profile but with significantly more mid-palate density and aging potential.

94e7d9b0-c1f7-4265-871f-cfe1ac037bed.description: It is a white wine designed for aging, and it is proving it, as it has held up very well over the years. Austere in its expression and full-bodied. A direct and tense palate with everything integrated; good body and a certain textural grip that supports a deep finish.
94e7d9b0-c1f7-4265-871f-cfe1ac037bed.rationale:
Core Value Proposition

    The "San Pablo" Benchmark: Sourced from the highest and coldest vineyard in Zuccardi's portfolio (1,400 meters). The extreme thermal amplitude results in a wine defined by electric acidity and extreme structural tension.

    Verticality & Purity: Vinified without malolactic fermentation or new oak. It represents the "unfiltered" expression of the Uco Valley's cold-climate Chardonnay, stripping away the buttery tropes of the variety.

    High Critical Standing: Typically holds 96–98 point scores (Luis Gutiérrez/Wine Advocate), placing it among the elite white wines of the Southern Hemisphere.

Market Positioning for Japan (Washoku Focus)

    The "Mineral Blade" for Sashimi: The wine's lack of malolactic fermentation results in a crisp, sharp acidity that acts as a precision tool for raw preparations. It is particularly effective with white-fleshed fish (Shiromi) and lean Maguro, where it highlights sweetness rather than masking it.

    Chalky Texture & Dashi Synergy: The "Fósil" name refers to the ancient marine deposits in the soil. This translates into a flinty, saline texture on the palate that resonates with the savory, oceanic depth of Dashi-based broths and Wanmono (suimono) dishes.

    A "Grand Cru" Alternative to Chablis: For high-end Tokyo and Osaka restaurants, Fósil offers the same lean, stony profile as a top-tier Chablis or Puligny-Montrachet but with the unique "mountain energy" of the Andes, providing a sophisticated talking point for discerning clientele.

73d8d593-856e-4d20-8344-0e44364d7ba4.description: Balanced aromas, as always classy yet contemporary. The palate is juicy and delicate, with fine grippy textures and well-integrated oak. It is an expressive wine that continues to show aging potential.
73d8d593-856e-4d20-8344-0e44364d7ba4.rationale:
Core Value Proposition

    The "Lafite of the Andes": A prestigious joint venture between Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite) and Catena Zapata. Petit Caro is the "second wine" of the estate, crafted with the same technical rigor and heritage as a Bordeaux Deuxième Vin.

    Bordeaux DNA, Argentine Spirit: A refined blend of 69% Malbec and 30% Cabernet Sauvignon (with 1% Ancellotta). It combines the structural elegance of Pauillac with the vibrant, sun-drenched fruit of high-altitude Mendoza.

    Masterful Aging: Aged for 12 months in French oak barrels produced by the Rothschild family's own cooperage (Tonnellerie des Domaines) in Pauillac, France.

Market Positioning for China (French Heritage Focus)

    The Rothschild Pedigree: In a market with a deep-rooted respect for the "Five First Growths," the DBR (Lafite) logo on the capsule and label provides immediate trust, status, and a "Grand Cru" quality guarantee for gifting and business banquets.

    A Familiar Stylistic Bridge: Unlike 100% Malbecs which can feel "too New World" for traditionalists, the significant Cabernet Sauvignon component and French oak treatment create a profile that Chinese collectors will find remarkably similar to a high-end Saint-Émilion or Médoc—offering "Lafite elegance" at a more accessible investment level.

    Symbolism of the Partnership: The "CARO" name (CAtena + ROthschild) represents a union of two "Royal Families" of wine. This narrative of long-term collaboration and mutual respect resonates deeply with Chinese values of lineage, partnership, and heritage.

37795c0d-9c9b-4840-98ab-f77d8dd08344.description: Because it combines the strength of Cabernet Sauvignon with the elegance of Merlot and the softness of Pinot Noir. Bordeaux and Burgundy in the same bottle. Its aromas are classic and balanced, with ripe nuances and hints of dried spices and leather. A direct palate, with fine tannins that define its passage and highlight its style. It possesses further aging potential.
37795c0d-9c9b-4840-98ab-f77d8dd08344.rationale:
Core Value Proposition

    A Living Legend: Established in 1934, Chateau Vieux is one of Argentina's oldest and most iconic brands. It is the definitive expression of "Estilo López" (The López Style)—a winemaking philosophy that prioritizes elegance, long-term aging, and a refusal to follow modern "fruit-bomb" trends.

    Masterful Tertiary Complexity: Unlike most modern reds, this wine undergoes an extraordinary 36 months (3 years) of aging in massive Nancy French oak vats (5,000 to 20,000 liters). This creates a sophisticated profile of leather, tobacco, and dried spices that mimics a perfectly cellared Bordeaux.

    The "Lynch-Bages" of Mendoza: Produced by the 5th generation of the López family, this estate-bottled Gran Reserva is a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon (dominant), Merlot, and Pinot Noir, offering a structural complexity rarely found in the Southern Hemisphere.

Market Positioning for China (French Heritage Focus)

    Visual Authority: The "Chateau" naming convention and the classic, European-inspired label design command immediate respect in China. It aligns with the aesthetic of Grand Vins, making it a high-value choice for corporate gifting and traditional banquets where "face" (status) is paramount.

    A "Museum Release" Experience: In a market that deeply values age and history, selling a "Gran Reserva" that has been aged for years before release is a significant competitive advantage. It positions the wine as a mature, collector-grade product rather than a "young" commercial export.

    The Refined Palate: The addition of Pinot Noir to the Cabernet-Merlot base provides a "silky" texture and "mahogany" nuances that appeal to the traditional Chinese appreciation for lighter-bodied, more aromatic, and structurally balanced French reds (reminiscent of aged Left-Bank Bordeaux or mature Rioja).

cddba4ec-4e15-4ec8-b361-fc5201bc1474.description: A luxury sparkling wine, based on Pinot Noir (80%) and Chardonnay (20%). Visually golden and bright, with a sustained perlage. Its complex aromas stand out thanks to the fine bubbles. Round and fresh, the notes of evolution are perceived on the palate in keeping with the house style.
cddba4ec-4e15-4ec8-b361-fc5201bc1474.rationale:
Core Value Proposition

    The Pinnacle of Argentine Fizz: The "Millésime" is Cruzat's flagship prestige cuvée, produced only in exceptional years. It is a traditional method (Champenoise) blend of 80% Pinot Noir and 20% Chardonnay from Gualtallary, the most sought-after sub-region in the Uco Valley.

    Extreme Aging: Undergoes an extraordinary 80 months (over 6.5 years) on the lees. This creates a level of autolytic complexity (brioche, toasted nuts, honey) that rivals "Grand Marque" Vintage Champagnes.

    High-Altitude Precision: Sourced from vineyards at 1,400 meters, providing a piercing natural acidity that remains the structural backbone of the wine even after nearly a decade of aging.

Market Positioning for Singapore (Tropical & Fine Dining)

    The "Tropical Humidity" Hero: In Singapore's relentless heat, high-dosage sparkling wines can feel cloying. Millésime 2018 is a Brut Nature/Extra Brut style (low dosage). Its bone-dry, saline, and mineral-driven finish provides a level of refreshment and "lift" that is essential for poolside luxury or outdoor terrace dining.

    The "Natural" Bridge for Michelin Bars: While technically traditional, Cruzat's focus on terroir purity and minimal intervention in the cellar appeals to Singapore's booming "Natural-adjacent" wine scene. It offers the "clean" profile that modern drinkers crave but with the sophisticated polish required by Michelin-starred establishments like Odette or Les Amis.

    Gastronomic Weight for Hawker-to-High-End: The 80 months of lees contact give this wine enough "umami" and weight to stand up to bold Singaporean flavors. It is an elite pairing for Chilli Crab (the acidity cuts the spice and fat) or Hainanese Chicken Rice (the ginger and yeast notes harmonize with the rice).

95f9a788-a7b1-438a-86cc-eb002684477a.description: Full-bodied, with ripe nuances and a certain creaminess. Very fresh, without direct white fruit and with just the right amount of yeast. It possesses great volume, and there is a persistence supported by evolution, with hints of dried fruits. Not a very deep finish but very tense, highlighting a delicate resinous quality. Mineral in its textures with very fine bubbles that unfold layers of flavors.
95f9a788-a7b1-438a-86cc-eb002684477a.rationale:
Core Value Proposition

    The "Moët Heritage" in Argentina: Baron B was founded by Baron Bertrand de Ladoucette (then President of Moët & Chandon) in 1959. "Héritage" is the ultimate expression of this French-Argentine lineage, representing the "Cuvée de Prestige" of the house.

    Non-Vintage Multi-Vintage (MV) Mastery: Edición 004 is a sophisticated blend of two distinct high-altitude harvests (2014 Chardonnay from Cepas del Plata and 2016 Pinot Noir from El Cepillo). This "Multi-Vintage" approach ensures maximum complexity and consistency.

    Extended Maturation: Aged for a minimum of 60 months (5 years) on the lees. This prolonged contact results in a fine, persistent perlage (bubble) and a creamy, gastronomic texture.

Market Positioning for Singapore (Tropical & Fine Dining)

    The "Club & Penthouse" Aesthetic: Baron B carries an aura of "Old World" European luxury that resonates with Singapore's high-society "Black Tie" events and elite members' clubs (e.g., Tanglin Club, 67 Pall Mall). The branding is synonymous with exclusive celebrations.

    A "Cooling" Structural Profile: Sourced from extreme altitudes (1,250–1,500 meters), the wine possesses a high-tension acidity that is vital for the Singaporean climate. It provides a "crisp" sensory experience that remains refreshing even in 80% humidity.

    Versatility for "Mod-Sin" Cuisine: The Pinot Noir dominance (El Cepillo) provides a red-fruit structure and savory spice that pairs exceptionally well with Modern Singaporean (Mod-Sin) dishes. It has the weight to complement Laksa-infused reductions or Smoked Duck, while the Chardonnay elegance handles delicate Dim Sum and Seafood Crudo.
